    About Bani Flow
    
  
      
  
      
    
      About Bani Flow
      
    
      Bani Flow maps the guru-shishya (teacher-student) lineage of Carnatic classical music — how ragas, compositions, and performance style pass between generations of musicians. Explore how different artists have rendered the same raga or composition to hear the living variety within a single tradition.
      
    
      Guru-Shishya View
      
    
      A directed graph of musicians. Edges flow guru → shishya. Tap a node once to see a brief preview; tap again to open the Musician panel. The filter chips at the top narrow the graph by era (colour-coded by period) or instrument (shape-coded by family).
      
    
      Mela-Janya View
      
    
      A wheel of the 72 melakarta ragas with their janya (derived) ragas branching outward. Tap a raga once to preview; tap again to open the Bani Flow panel, which lists all recordings on that raga. Use raga name links to navigate across ragas.
      
    
      Musician Panel
      
    
      Shows the repertoire of a selected musician — concert recordings grouped by performance, each tagged with a composition and raga. Tap the ▶ button on any recording to open it in the YouTube player.
      
    
      Bani Flow Panel
      
    
      Search for a raga or composition in the left sidebar to see every musician who has recorded it, in order of recency. Compare how a guru and their students each render Thodi or Bhairavi — this is the heart of the exploration.
      
    
      YouTube Player
      
    
      Opens in a floating window (desktop) or a slide-up strip (mobile). Click the artist chip in the player bar to jump to that musician in the graph. The tracklist button (☰) lets you switch tracks within a concert.
      
    
      E-Tanpura
      
    
      The row of note buttons in the header plays an electronic tanpura drone for that sruti (tonic pitch). Use it as a pitch reference while listening or singing along. Click the lit button again — or the ● power indicator — to stop the drone.
